$comment: c111 (D-RUNTIME-PE-MAIN-ARGS): the WIDE program-entry RUN witness (the wmain(int,wchar_t**) counterpart of the narrow main_argc_argv). On the pe64 target the Windows OS entry receives NO C argument vector, so the MIR-tier synthesized pre-main init (synthesizePeStartup) calls the msvcrt WIDE arg-fetch export __wgetmainargs -- chosen over the narrow __getmainargs by the argv ELEMENT width (unsigned short = pe wide-char u16) -- to populate argc/argv and forwards them to wmain. The runner spawns with no extra args -> wmain must see argc==1 + a non-empty WIDE argv[0] (argv[0][0] is its first u16 code unit) + argv[1]==NULL -> exit 42; each failed layer exits a distinct 90..94 (see main.c). pe64-ONLY: wmain+wide-argv is a Windows construct; the ELF stack-vector mechanism hands a NARROW char** vector, a category mismatch for a wide entry, so no ELF target is declared. RED-on-disable: remove `processArgs` from the pe64 shipped exec JSON (runtime-read config) -> the synth never runs -> wmain reads garbage entry-register argc -> exit 90 instead of 42.
